claim_status: no-claim page-surface composition inventory only; no UI-GATE-7 claim, no page-surface approval, no compositor ownership decision, no UI toolkit selection, no renderer-texture composition proof, no typed page-surface handle implementation, no brokered surface handle proof, no resize scale or damage proof, no input IME or accessibility routing proof, no occlusion or capture proof, no renderer-crash or GPU device-loss proof, no software-fallback proof, no latency or frame-pacing proof, no release-path UI approval, and no production claim

surface_contract_fields.purpose: Bind the page surface to the Turing view that owns presentation, input routing, accessibility composition, capture policy, and resource attribution.
surface_contract_fields.missing_evidence: No generated surface protocol, page-surface handle type, or stale-view rejection test exists.

surface_contract_fields.purpose: Reject stale renderer frames, hit-test state, accessibility subtrees, input targets, and release acknowledgements after navigation commit.
surface_contract_fields.missing_evidence: No document-epoch checked surface protocol or stale-epoch negative fixture exists.

surface_contract_fields.purpose: Reject stale GPU resources and synchronize page-surface recreation after GPU device loss or backend reset.
surface_contract_fields.missing_evidence: No device-generation protocol, GPU-loss fixture, or resource-rebuild trace exists.

surface_contract_fields.purpose: Describe CSS/layout-space bounds for hit testing, accessibility bounds, zoom, and chrome/page composition.
surface_contract_fields.missing_evidence: No resize or scale fixture records logical-size changes.

surface_contract_fields.purpose: Describe device-pixel allocation size so textures, damage, capture, and presentation are bounded before GPU use.
surface_contract_fields.missing_evidence: No texture allocation or oversized-surface rejection evidence exists.

surface_contract_fields.purpose: Bind page textures, chrome geometry, input coordinates, and accessibility bounds to the platform scale factor.
surface_contract_fields.missing_evidence: No multi-scale fixture or scale-change trace exists.

surface_contract_fields.purpose: Keep page and chrome composition color-correct and auditable across software fallback and GPU paths.
surface_contract_fields.missing_evidence: No color-space fixture, metadata record, or fallback comparison exists.

surface_contract_fields.purpose: Prevent transparent or premultiplied page content from confusing trusted chrome, capture, or hit-test regions.
surface_contract_fields.missing_evidence: No alpha-mode fixture or trusted-chrome overlay test exists.

surface_contract_fields.purpose: Bound repaint and composite work while preferring conservative over missing damage.
surface_contract_fields.missing_evidence: No damage-region trace, damaged-frame replay, or missed-damage negative case exists.

surface_contract_fields.purpose: Coordinate renderer frame readiness, GPU resource ownership, and release without exposing raw renderer or native handles to trusted UI.
surface_contract_fields.missing_evidence: No synchronization primitive decision, timeout behavior, or handle-broker evidence exists.

surface_contract_fields.purpose: Order frames, release acknowledgements, input snapshots, and presentation deadlines without accepting stale or replayed frames.
surface_contract_fields.missing_evidence: No frame-sequence replay, drop, reorder, or late-frame fixture exists.

surface_contract_fields.purpose: Support bounded latency and frame pacing for page and chrome composition without hiding missed-frame attribution.
surface_contract_fields.missing_evidence: No input-to-present, deadline-miss, or frame-pacing trace exists.

surface_contract_fields.purpose: Return GPU and shared resources deterministically without leaking resources or freeing live page textures too early.
surface_contract_fields.missing_evidence: No release-ack timeout, duplicate ack, or resource-leak fixture exists.

surface_contract_fields.purpose: Ensure the UI receives only a brokered page-surface handle instead of a renderer process pointer or unchecked native GPU handle.
surface_contract_fields.missing_evidence: No brokered surface handle implementation, privilege review, or raw-handle denial case exists.

composition_alternatives.name: Turing-Owned Window Swapchain
composition_alternatives.ownership_model: Turing owns the window swapchain and composites toolkit chrome plus page textures through a Turing-owned compositor path.
required_evidence: resize, scale, damage, and device-loss traces
composition_alternatives.missing_evidence: No toolkit adapter, swapchain prototype, external texture import, or fallback path exists.

composition_alternatives.name: Toolkit External Texture Hook
composition_alternatives.ownership_model: The selected toolkit owns the window surface and exposes a stable external-texture or custom-render hook behind a Turing-owned adapter.
required_evidence: input, IME, accessibility, capture, and occlusion fixtures
composition_alternatives.missing_evidence: No toolkit is selected and no external-texture hook has been proven stable enough for browser chrome.

composition_alternatives.name: Platform Child Surface
composition_alternatives.ownership_model: Platform child surfaces are used only where unavoidable and kept behind a Turing-owned semantic contract.
required_evidence: security review for cross-origin and trusted chrome boundaries
composition_alternatives.missing_evidence: No platform child-surface prototype or security exception record exists.

composition_alternatives.name: Deterministic Software Fallback
composition_alternatives.ownership_model: A software composition path preserves correctness, screenshots, tests, recovery, and reduced hardware assumptions when GPU presentation fails.
composition_alternatives.missing_evidence: No software fallback compositor, comparison harness, or GPU-loss fallback result exists.

workflow_test_matrix.required_observation: Renderer-produced page textures and trusted chrome resize together without stale dimensions, missing damage, or authority confusion.
Scale factor changes preserve page texture quality, chrome geometry, input coordinate mapping, and accessibility bounds.
Damage regions are conservative, bounded, sequenced, and never omit required page or chrome repaint.
Hit testing selects trusted chrome or page surface before dispatch while preserving view, frame, document epoch, and user-activation identity.
required_artifacts: trusted chrome versus page target transcript

workflow_test_matrix.required_observation: IME composition, candidate windows, selection, and committed text target the current focused document or trusted chrome field only.
Trusted chrome and renderer-produced accessibility subtrees compose without exposing hidden cross-origin or stale-document data.
Occluded page surfaces reduce work only when trusted chrome, input, capture, and accessibility behavior remain correct.
Capture includes or excludes trusted chrome, page surfaces, protected content, and hidden regions according to explicit policy.
Renderer crash replaces only the page surface with accessible recovery UI and does not corrupt trusted chrome or replay actions.
GPU device loss rebuilds toolkit and page resources, increments device generation, and preserves session and accessibility state.
Input-to-present latency, frame pacing, missed-frame attribution, and release acknowledgements remain bounded under page/chrome composition.

failure_recovery_cases.safe_result: reject stale frame, hit-test, input, accessibility, and release-ack data before presentation or dispatch
reject stale or wrong-view surface handles and return a bounded error
failure_recovery_cases.missing_evidence: No wrong-view or closed-view handle test exists.

failure_recovery_cases.safe_result: reject stale GPU resources after device loss and require a rebuilt brokered surface handle
replace only the page surface with accessible recovery UI and keep trusted chrome responsive
rebuild GPU and toolkit resources or fall back to software without discarding the session model
failure_recovery_cases.missing_evidence: No GPU device-loss rebuild or software-fallback fixture exists.

failure_recovery_cases.safe_result: stop or restart the UI adapter without corrupting profile state or replaying consequential commands
failure_recovery_cases.missing_evidence: No toolkit failure injection or session-protection evidence exists.

failure_recovery_cases.safe_result: cancel or reclaim bounded resources without freeing live surfaces or leaking GPU memory indefinitely
failure_recovery_cases.missing_evidence: No release-ack timeout or duplicate-ack fixture exists.

failure_recovery_cases.safe_result: deny pixel sampling, accessibility leakage, and metadata confusion across origin, site, frame, process, and document boundaries
failure_recovery_cases.missing_evidence: No cross-origin hidden-surface or unauthorized sampling negative test exists.

failure_recovery_cases.safe_result: deny or redact capture of protected page, trusted chrome, and hidden surfaces according to policy
failure_recovery_cases.missing_evidence: No capture policy denial matrix or redaction artifact exists.

security_identity_boundaries.unsafe_if_missing: A page surface or capture path could mix resources or recovery state across profiles.
security_identity_boundaries.missing_evidence: No profile-aware surface or capture fixture exists.

security_identity_boundaries.enforced_identity: ViewId issued by the browser kernel for the active window, tab, split pane, or page view
security_identity_boundaries.unsafe_if_missing: A stale or wrong page surface could present into another tab, split pane, or window.
security_identity_boundaries.missing_evidence: No wrong-view surface handle test exists.

security_identity_boundaries.enforced_identity: DocumentEpoch assigned at navigation commit and checked on every frame, hit-test, input, accessibility, and release path
security_identity_boundaries.unsafe_if_missing: Old frames, hit tests, accessibility nodes, or release acknowledgements could survive navigation.
The UI could trust a renderer process pointer or unchecked native handle.
security_identity_boundaries.missing_evidence: No brokered-surface-handle or raw-handle denial proof exists.

security_identity_boundaries.enforced_identity: Origin, TopLevelSite, FrameId, and site-instance ownership
security_identity_boundaries.unsafe_if_missing: Cross-origin or hidden frame content could leak through pixels, accessibility nodes, hit testing, or capture metadata.

security_identity_boundaries.enforced_identity: DeviceGeneration assigned when GPU or software composition resources are created or rebuilt
security_identity_boundaries.unsafe_if_missing: Stale GPU resources could be reused after device loss or backend reset.

security_identity_boundaries.enforced_identity: Brokered page-surface handle issued by the compositor or GPU service instead of raw renderer or native handles
security_identity_boundaries.unsafe_if_missing: Renderer or toolkit code could pass raw native GPU handles around the broker.
security_identity_boundaries.missing_evidence: No brokered handle protocol or denial case exists.

security_identity_boundaries.enforced_identity: semantic resource owner for CPU, memory, GPU, wakeups, energy, and frame work
security_identity_boundaries.unsafe_if_missing: Page, chrome, GPU, and shared resources could become unaccounted or misattributed under pressure.

evidence_blockers.missing_evidence: No schema, generated type, or protocol decision defines the page-surface contract.
No brokered surface handle, privilege review, or raw-handle denial fixture exists.
No Slint, Vizia, Floem, GPUI, or owner-approved reduced adapter executes the same page-surface tasks.
No simulated renderer frame source, texture stream, or frame-sequence fixture exists.
No combined hit-test, input, IME, accessibility, and page-tree composition fixture exists.

evidence_blockers.blocker: resize scale damage occlusion capture fixtures
evidence_blockers.missing_evidence: No resize, scale, damage, occlusion, or capture fixture output exists.
No renderer crash, GPU device-loss, toolkit failure, or software-fallback fault fixture exists.
No p50, p95, p99 latency trace, frame-pacing distribution, or deadline-miss artifact exists.
No UI-runtime, compositor, GPU, platform, accessibility, security, performance, quality, release, or architecture owner has accepted page-surface evidence or ADR-0016.

unsupported_boundaries: no resize scale or damage proof
no input IME or accessibility routing proof
no renderer-crash or GPU device-loss proof
